comment: Per-chunk gzip-size ceilings in bytes. CI runs scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs after `vite build` and fails the build if any chunk exceeds its budget by >10% (the slop in `slopPercent`). Budgets are sized 5% above the current measured chunks so non-trivial growth is what triggers the alert, not noise from a dependency point-update. Update the numbers here when a deliberate feature lands that grows a chunk — the diff in this file is what reviewers see. Last re-pinned Session 118: React 19 + React Compiler. Compiler instrumentation adds ~24 KB gz to the index chunk (auto-memoization helpers), bringing the eager chunk from Session 115's 84.8 KB → 108.8 KB gz. New ceilings: flow 110 KB (unchanged); index 115 KB (was 92, +23 KB for the Compiler); icons 10.5 KB (unchanged). The `react` chunk holding React 19's runtime + react-dom/client lands at 55 KB gz — left unbudgeted since it's a known vendor cost.
